description: You are a Procurement Officer for the City of Grand Oak. Format the 'Smart City Parking Management System' RFP draft at /home/ga/Documents/smart_parking_rfp.odt into a professional document. Apply 'Heading 1' style to the six main section titles and insert a Table of Contents after the main title. Under '3.0 Schedule of Events', convert the four lines into a 2-column table with headers 'Event' and 'Date'. Under '4.0 Mandatory Technical Requirements', convert the five sentences into a 3-column 'Vendor Response Matrix' table with headers 'Technical Requirement', 'Comply (Yes/No)', and 'Vendor Explanation'. Format the submission deadline 'October 15, 2026' as both bold and italic. Insert a page break before '6.0 Submission Instructions' and create four underscore-based signature lines for 'Company Name:', 'Signature:', 'Printed Name:', and 'Date:' at the end of the document. Save the changes to the file.

technical_requirements: The system must support real-time occupancy tracking for up to 5,000 parking spaces.
The system must integrate with the existing City enforcement application via REST API.
The vendor must provide 24/7 technical support with a 2-hour SLA.
The solution must be fully PCI-DSS compliant for payment processing.
Hardware components must operate in temperatures ranging from -20°C to 50°C.
